data.question: Is the QRS duration prolonged on this ECG? Note that a QRS duration is considered to be prolonged if it is greater than 120 milliseconds.
In which of the following segments can you observe a QRS wave that demonstrates the prolonged QRS duration? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG strip, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured QRS duration fall into? The ranges are inclusive of the lower bound but exclusive of the upper bound.
Does this ECG show a dominant S wave in leads V1 and V2, where the amplitude of the negative deflection in the QRS complex is greater than the amplitude of the positive deflection?
